prerequisites: A DingConnect account with access to the Account Settings section.
An API key generated from the Developer tab under Account Settings in your DingConnect account (used for the api_key field).
A start date (start_date) in ISO 8601 UTC format matching the pattern YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, used as the replication start point.

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a single DingConnect API key supplied in the api_key secret field. The connector sends it as an HTTP request header (ApiKeyAuthenticator, header field name api_key) on every call to https://api.dingconnect.com. Generate the key from the Developer tab in the Account Settings section of your DingConnect account.

setupSteps.title: Generate a DingConnect API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your DingConnect account, open Account Settings, and go to the Developer tab. Generate an API key there. This key is used for the required api_key field and is sent as an HTTP header to authenticate requests against the DingConnect API at https://api.dingconnect.com.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key in the API Key (api_key) field. It is required and stored as a secret. This is the only credential the connector needs to authenticate.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Enter the required Start date (start_date) as an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp in the form YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (for example 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). This defines the point from which data is replicated.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set a correlation header
setupSteps.body: Optionally provide an X-Correlation-Id value. This non-secret header is sent with requests to correlate HTTP traffic between client and server. Leave it blank if you do not need request tracing.

sources.title: Airbyte source-ding-connect manifest.yaml (authenticator, base URL, and config spec)
